question_text: A washing machine uses 2400 J of electrical energy and wastes 600 J as heat and sound. Calculate the efficiency of the washing machine and explain which energy transfer is considered 'useful' in this case.
correct_answer: Efficiency = 75%. The useful energy transfer is the mechanical energy used to wash the clothes (1800 J), while the heat and sound (600 J) are wasted.
explanation: Efficiency = (useful energy ÷ total energy) × 100. The useful energy is 2400 - 600 = 1800 J (the work done washing clothes), so efficiency = (1800 ÷ 2400) × 100 = 75%. The heat and sound are unwanted energy losses that don't help with washing, so they count as wasted energy.
